title: Jet alone
wikitext:
[]: Jet alone.             Quote Post by Dascoo » Mon Nov 27, 2006 7:37 am Jet alone cannot generate an ATF, so how was it supposed to defend itself against angel attacks?

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Nov 27, 2006 7:54 am I bet the people who made it didn't have any idea of the battle potential of the Angels. It would have been funny if Jet Alone actually got to fight an angel such as Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Dascoo » Mon Nov 27, 2006 7:57 am Cut to pieces....many many pieces. But I would imagine that they would go limited production, such as possibly 6 attacking at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Mon Nov 27, 2006 9:27 am Jet Alone wasn't the final production model, presumably. They mention at one point that they were looking into ways of simulating AT Fields. Not that they would have succeeded, but hey. What I want to know is why they thought sending a weapon that's powered by a nuclear reactor against monsters that rend shit apart was a good idea.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 27, 2006 10:14 am I believe that guy said it was "only a matter of time". He was over-confident.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 27, 2006 2:05 pm The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core". Then again, JA isn't made of PWN, so Tokita would also have to solve the problem of amplifying the faux-ATF, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Nov 28, 2006 3:24 am Reichu wrote: The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core". Then again, JA isn't made of PWN, so Tokita would also have to solve the problem of amplifying the faux-ATF, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Tue Nov 28, 2006 7:45 am Well, it seemed the ATF was a close guarded NERV secret that they might not have known about till late in the project. Jet Alone could very well have worked if they found a way around the ATF.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Nov 28, 2006 5:00 pm Reichu wrote: The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core".

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Dec 25, 2006 6:49 am Reichu wrote: The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core".

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 25, 2006 4:05 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Reichu wrote: The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core".

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Mon Dec 25, 2006 4:09 pm Ornette wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Reichu wrote: The dummy system actually functions as an "artificial soul". So, in theory, Tokita's wish could have come true, if that kind of technology were implemented to give JA a functional faux-ATF-generating "core".

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 25, 2006 4:54 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Is NERV's crazy soul magic technology known to anyone but themselves?

[]:             Quote Post by brainDamage » Mon Dec 25, 2006 8:39 pm Jet Alone = Walking N2 mine

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 25, 2006 9:30 pm brainDamage wrote: Jet Alone = Walking N2 mine

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Dec 26, 2006 12:43 am brainDamage wrote: Jet Alone = Walking nuclear explosion

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 12:36 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I don't think so. The dummy system appears to simply function as an artifical pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 31, 2006 2:00 pm Jet Alone would have been cooler if its role was something besides "lol out of control walking nuke" and it looked like the Improved JA from the NGE2 game. JA could have gone against an Angel and gotten its ass kicked or something, just make the point that only Evas can kill Angels clear in a cooler way.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Dec 31, 2006 2:40 pm Actually the fact that gendo bothered to have jetalone sabotaged may suggest that the end product of their ATF research may not have been as useless as everyone here thinks. If it really stood no chance of working there would be no need to sabotage it, just wait until they try to send it against an angel and it'll get its big metal ass kicked. How they would have gotten the end product of jetalone to stand a chance is a mystery, but the people at nerve that bothered to sabotage it at least seem to have considered jetalone a serious threat to their interests.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Dec 31, 2006 7:00 pm Defectron wrote: Actually the fact that gendo bothered to have jetalone sabotaged may suggest that the end product of their ATF research may not have been as useless as everyone here thinks. If it really stood no chance of working there would be no need to sabotage it, just wait until they try to send it against an angel and it'll get its big metal ass kicked. How they would have gotten the end product of jetalone to stand a chance is a mystery, but the people at nerve that bothered to sabotage it at least seem to have considered jetalone a serious threat to their interests.
